pr.title: Split up some of the interface code from text_4
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly-only organization PR that carved interface/HUD-related code out of the monolithic asm/melee/text_4.s file into dedicated asm/melee/if modules, then registered those new object files in obj_files.mk before text_4.o. The evidence points to a mechanical split/rebucket rather than C decompilation or behavior changes: text_4.s lost 11085 lines, new asm/melee/if/*.s files gained the corresponding code, and obj_files.mk added the new objects.

key_files.role: source monolith reduced by removing the interface-code block
new split-out interface stock assembly module
new split-out interface status assembly module
new split-out interface magnify assembly module
new split-out interface time assembly module
new split-out common/all interface assembly module
new address-named split-out assembly module for still-unnamed interface code
build manifest updated to include the new interface object files before text_4.o

what_changed: Added six new assembly files under asm/melee/if/: ifall.s, iftime.s, ifstatus.s, code_802F7288.s, ifstock.s, and ifmagnify.s.
Added obj_files.mk entries for the new interface object files in TEXT_O_FILES immediately before asm/melee/text_4.o.

The new files preserve raw assembly style with macros.inc includes, .section .text declarations, address comments, and existing func_/lbl_ symbols such as func_802F7288, func_802F77F8, and lbl_802F73C4.
No PR body, issue comments, reviews, or review comments were present in the provided slice, so there is no explicit rationale beyond the title and diff.
smart_moves: Reduced the scope of the generic text_4.s bucket by moving identifiable interface code into a domain-specific asm/melee/if/ directory.
Kept build order explicit by adding the new object files before text_4.o, which is important for preserving section layout during incremental decomp and assembly splits.
Used meaningful module names where the subsystem was identifiable, such as ifstock, ifstatus, ifmagnify, iftime, and ifall.
Left an unclear chunk as code_802F7288.s rather than forcing an unsupported semantic name; this keeps the split useful without overclaiming understanding.
Preserved address-based labels and raw assembly content, minimizing risk that an organizational split would introduce matching or behavior changes.
decomp_lessons: When a monolithic assembly file contains identifiable subsystem regions, splitting those regions into domain folders can make later decompilation and symbol work more searchable without requiring immediate C conversion.
For assembly-only rebucketing, preserve object order in obj_files.mk; the order of new objects relative to the remaining monolith is part of the match strategy.
Use address-based filenames like code_802F7288.s for partially understood chunks instead of inventing semantic names before the code’s purpose is proven.
A successful split can be judged partly by near-balanced removal/addition counts and by keeping original labels, comments, and section annotations intact.
The PR demonstrates an incremental cleanup step: improve source layout first, then leave future decompilation to target smaller module files.
naming_conventions: Interface assembly modules were placed under asm/melee/if/.
Short subsystem-prefixed filenames used lower-case if* names: ifall.s, iftime.s, ifstatus.s, ifstock.s, ifmagnify.s.
Unknown or insufficiently named code used the address-based pattern code_802F7288.s.
Existing unresolved symbols remained in the project’s address-style conventions, including func_802F7288 and lbl_802F73C4.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This was a raw assembly split, not a C matching PR.
Existing address comments and func_/lbl_ labels were preserved across the move.
obj_files.mk was updated so the newly split interface objects assemble before the residual asm/melee/text_4.o.
The diff shows large deletes from text_4.s and large adds to new asm files, consistent with moving assembly while preserving code bytes/order rather than rewriting logic.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer naming, struct, header, or matching feedback can be extracted from the available evidence.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile asm/melee/if/ifstock.s or asm/melee/if/ifstatus.s into C.
Search for when code_802F7288.s received a semantic module name or C source equivalent.
Check whether later obj_files.mk changes preserve or alter the ordering of the interface objects added in PR 228.
Look for later symbol naming work around func_802F7288, func_802F77F8, and lbl_803F9628.
